reasoning: FIDE updates classical rankings exactly once a month (on the 1st of each month). The Wikipedia page for FIDE rankings mirrors these official lists. As of October 31, 2025, Wikipedia shows the November 1, 2025 list with Fabiano Caruana's rating at 2795. Since Wikipedia will not be updated with officially certified monthly ratings until December 1, Caruana's rating will still be listed as 2795 on November 16. A 1% increase over 2795 corresponds to reaching an Elo rating of roughly 2823. Thus, on Nov 16, the likelihood is effectively 0 (bounded to 0.02). To reach 2823 by December 9 or February 7, Caruana would have to gain 28 rating points at an already elite level (~2800). Gains of this magnitude typically require winning an incredibly strong tournament (or multiple) with almost no losses, which is highly improbable in a single month and remains very unlikely over a 3-month period. Forecasting a slow, widening uncertainty (0.02 -> 0.03 -> 0.06) as time increases.
